title: rosa villosa var. sancti-andreae
intro: Rosa villosa var. sancti-andreae, also known as the Andean wild rose, is a species of rose native to the Andean region of South America, characterized by its fragrant flowers and thorny stems. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and fever. The health benefits of Rosa villosa var. sancti-andreae include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce menstrual cramps, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
